creator: cem@freebsd.org
text:
If there's an operation for flushing on each close, and it can report an error status (sounds like yes and yes), I think we should invoke it and propagate the error in our FUSE VOP_CLOSE().  Regardless of other VOP_CLOSE implementations.

(OTOH, many VOP_CLOSE *callers* ignore the return value of VOP_CLOSE, but importantly vn_close1 <- vn_close <- vn_closefile <- fo_close <- _fdrop <- closef <- closefp <- kern_close propagates errors all the way from VOP_CLOSE to close(2).)
